title: Investing in quality: The benefits of well-made furniture and art
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Interior designer Kate Maloney uses locally made quality furniture and art in her Cambridge project.
Maloney repurposed furnishings from a previous Somerville project, emphasizing craftsmanship and sustainability.
Tracy Glover Studio's hand-blown glass lighting was adapted for different kitchen settings.

title: ‘He drives them’: New Bruins forward Lukas Reichel made an instant impact at TD Garden
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lukas Reichel scored a goal and an assist in his debut for the Boston Bruins in a 6-1 win over the Winnipeg Jets.
The Bruins acquired Reichel from the Vancouver Canucks for a 2026 sixth-round pick near the NHL trade deadline.
Boston is tied for first wild-card spot in the Eastern Conference with 84 points, needing more production from their third line.

title: Ask the Remodeler: How to keep melted snow out of your basement
author: Mark Philben -- Globe Correspondent
bullets: Melted snow and spring rains can lead to water seeping into basements, requiring careful preventative measures.
Short-term solutions include clearing snow from basement windows, ensuring downspouts are clear, and repairing rusted bulkhead covers.
Long-term solutions involve installing French drains and sump pumps to manage and divert water away from basements effectively.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge of recent events in Greater Boston.
Peter Chianca has been the general assignment editor at Boston.com since 2019.
Chianca is also a seasoned news editor, columnist, and music writer in Greater Boston.

title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers voted to authorize a strike amid stalled contract negotiations with management.
The union was recognized by management in June 2024, representing 40 employees affiliated with UAW Local 1596.
Key union concerns include job protection and the impact of self-service kiosks on employment.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora predicts Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 season.
FanGraphs projects no Red Sox player will hit 20 or more home runs in the 2026 season.
Wilyer Abreu hit 22 home runs in 115 games last season and excelled in the 2026 World Baseball Classic.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots may draft offensive tackle Max Iheanachor from Arizona State with the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Iheanachor is praised for his athleticism and potential, having played well at East Los Angeles Community College and Arizona State.
The Patriots aim to strengthen offensive tackle depth, considering Morgan Moses is 35 and Will Campbell recently struggled.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester school officials denounced racist social media comments after a South High vs. Andover basketball game.
NAACP and YWCA condemned threats and racial slurs targeting South High players, calling them unacceptable and dehumanizing.
The incident involved a viral video and prompted demands for a community stand against racism and cyberbullying.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, from Rehoboth, charged with four counts of sexual exploitation of children by federal authorities.
Authorities found child sexual abuse material on Mota's phone during a border search at Logan Airport.
Mota admitted interaction with 20-25 minors, exchanging hundreds of illegal images via text, Snapchat, and Telegram.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened after water main break caused extensive flooding.
Boston Water and Sewer Commission identified and repaired a 12-inch pipe rupture causing the flooding.
Several businesses, including daycares, were evacuated due to the flooding until repairs were completed.
